question.text: A designated person of a listed company bought 10,000 shares at Rs. 200 per share and sold them at Rs. 240 per share within four months. Under Schedule B of SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015, what amount of profit is liable to be disgorged for credit to the Investor Protection and Education Fund?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The contra trade restriction period is at least six months. Since the designated person sold within four months, this is a contra trade violation. Profit = (240 - 200) x 10,000 = Rs. 4,00,000, which must be disgorged to IEPF. **The Trap:** Rs. 2,00,000 (cost) and Rs. 2,40,000 (sale proceeds) are not the profit amount. The profit per share is Rs. 40, total Rs. 4,00,000. **Cross-Exam Context:** Calculation of disgorgement amounts is tested in NISM Series III-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The contra trade restriction of six months applies regardless of whether the person was in possession of UPSI at the time of trading.
